questions.question: What is a force?
questions.explanation: A force is defined as a push or pull acting on an object.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of applying force?
questions.explanation: Pushing a door involves applying a force.

questions.question: Which force pulls objects toward the Earth?
questions.explanation: Gravity pulls objects toward the Earth.

questions.question: Which force acts between two magnets?
questions.explanation: Magnets exert magnetic force on each other.

questions.question: Which force acts when two surfaces rub against each other?
questions.explanation: Friction occurs when two surfaces move against each other.

questions.question: Which of the following forces requires contact?
questions.explanation: Muscular force requires physical contact with the object.

questions.question: Which force slows down moving objects?
questions.explanation: Friction acts opposite to motion and slows objects down.

questions.question: Which force allows us to walk without slipping?
questions.explanation: Friction between shoes and the ground allows us to walk.

questions.question: Which type of force does not require physical contact?
questions.explanation: Non-contact forces act without direct contact.

questions.question: Which of the following is a non-contact force?
questions.explanation: Gravity acts without physical contact.

questions.question: What happens when a force is applied to an object?
questions.explanation: Force can change shape, speed or direction of an object.

questions.question: Which force pulls a falling apple to the ground?
questions.explanation: Gravity pulls objects toward the Earth.

questions.question: Which example shows muscular force?

options.D: A balloon sticking to a wall

questions.explanation: Muscular force is produced by muscles when lifting objects.

questions.question: Which force acts between charged objects?
questions.explanation: Electrostatic force acts between electrically charged objects.

questions.question: Which force helps vehicles stop when brakes are applied?
questions.explanation: Friction between brake pads and wheels slows the vehicle.

questions.question: Which type of force occurs when we pull a rope?
questions.explanation: Pulling a rope uses muscular force.

questions.question: Which force keeps planets in orbit around the Sun?
questions.explanation: Gravity keeps planets moving in orbit.

questions.question: Which example shows friction?
questions.explanation: Rubbing surfaces together produces friction.

questions.question: Which of the following forces can act at a distance?
questions.explanation: Magnetic force acts without physical contact.

questions.question: Which of the following is a contact force?
questions.explanation: Friction occurs only when two surfaces are in contact.

questions.question: What is needed to move a stationary object?
questions.explanation: A force is required to move an object at rest.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of gravitational force?

options.B: An apple falling from a tree

questions.explanation: Gravity pulls objects toward the Earth.

questions.question: Which force acts when we push a box across the floor?
questions.explanation: Friction acts between the box and the floor.

questions.question: Which example shows non-contact force?
questions.explanation: Magnetic force acts without direct contact.

questions.question: What can force change in an object?
questions.explanation: Force can change the shape, speed or direction of an object.
